contentHtml:
What is this page?

This Help Center is the single entry point for user guidance. You can browse categories, search by keyword, or open context help using a section and operation.

How context help works

When your page provides a section and operation ID, the system resolves help in this order:

  1. Exact match: section + operation
  2. Section default: section only
  3. Global default: this article

Tips

  • Use search for quick results.
  • Use tags to narrow down topics.
  • Check related articles at the bottom of each page.
summary: How to use the integrated help center and find context-specific guidance.
title: Help Center Overview

contentHtml:
Before saving

  • Select a valid department.
  • Set class code and class name.
  • Confirm term and program mapping as required.

Validation flow

The form validates required fields before submit. In wizard mode, users can save or cancel from any step.

After save

Review generated records and verify linked program and term references.
summary: Specific instructions for creating classes and validating required fields.
title: Create Class (Operation Guide)

contentHtml:
Classes setup

Use the Classes form for standard entry and the Classes wizard for guided setup. Department selection depends on valid department records and matching picker endpoint setup.

Common issues

  • Picker returns non-JSON response: verify route returns JSON for X-AJAX-Request.
  • No department results: confirm departments exist and search fields are configured.
  • Wizard step visibility: ensure step configuration only renders fields for the active step.
summary: General guidance for managing classes forms, wizards, and pickers.
title: Classes Module Help

contentHtml:
Feature purpose

Request Rate Monitor tracks request bursts and can enforce limits. It is designed for gradual rollout from monitor mode to enforce mode.

Monitor vs enforce

If logs show DENIED but monitorOnly is true, the request exceeded limit but was not blocked. To block, use enforce mode at route or group level.

Policy levels

  • Website policy defines global defaults and route catalog.
  • Organization policy can define custom routes.
  • Exact route rules are evaluated before group rules.

Troubleshooting quick checks

  1. Confirm requestControl.enabled is true.
  2. Verify path/method/matchType are correct.
  3. Check if route mode is enforce or inherits monitor.
  4. Inspect logs for routeSpecific and rateLimitGroup.
summary: Understand monitor vs enforce behavior, phases, route overrides, and troubleshooting.
title: Request Rate Monitor
